sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "Highly effective for local control of cutaneous lymphomas; used with curative intent for limited disease and palliatively for symptomatic lesions.", "principles": ["Radiation therapy (RT) is extremely radiosensitive in cutaneous lymphomas.", "Involved-site radiation therapy (ISRT) is the standard approach, targeting clinically evident disease with adequate margins (1-2 cm circumferentially and in depth).", "Dose and fractionation depend on disease subtype, extent, and intent (curative vs. palliative).", "RT can be used alone for limited disease or combined with skin-directed or systemic therapies for more extensive disease."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Curative RT for indolent PCBCL (PCMZL, PCFCL)", "total_dose": "24 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "Conventional fractionation (e.g., 2 Gy per fraction)", "fractions": "12 fractions", "schedule": "Daily over 2-3 weeks", "indication": "Solitary/regional disease"}, {"name": "Palliative RT for MF/SS", "total_dose": "8-12 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "Up to 8 Gy in a single fraction; lower doses per fraction (3-5 Gy) may be preferred", "fractions": "1-4 fractions", "schedule": "Over 1-2 weeks", "indication": "Individual patches, plaques, or tumors for symptom control"}, {"name": "Curative RT for unilesional MF", "total_dose": "24-30 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "Conventional fractionation", "fractions": "12-15 fractions", "schedule": "Over 2-3 weeks", "indication": "Rare initial unilesional presentation"}, {"name": "RT for PC-ALCL", "total_dose": "24 Gy (curative); doses as low as 6 Gy used", "dose_per_fraction": "Conventional fractionation", "fractions": "Varies", "schedule": "", "indication": "Solitary or localized lesions"}, {"name": "RT for SPTCL", "total_dose": "Up to 40 Gy (adequate dose uncertain)", "dose_per_fraction": "Conventional fractionation", "fractions": "Varies", "schedule": "", "indication": "Single lesion or limited disease"}, {"name": "Total skin electron beam therapy (TSEBT)", "total_dose": "12-36 Gy (conventional dose ≥30 Gy; low dose <30 Gy)", "dose_per_fraction": "4-6 Gy per week", "fractions": "Varies", "schedule": "Over 2-9 weeks", "indication": "Generalized skin involvement (MF/SS)"}], "approaches": [{"name": "ISRT for cutaneous lesions", "dose_fractionation": "Conventional fractionation (2 Gy per fraction) or hypofractionated regimens", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not typically used concurrently for cutaneous lymphomas", "indication": "Localized patches, plaques, tumors of MF/SS; PCBCL; PC-ALCL", "key_trial": "", "toxicities": "Skin erythema, desquamation, fatigue; long-term risks include skin atrophy, telangiectasia, and secondary malignancies (low risk with modern techniques)"}, {"name": "TSEBT", "dose_fractionation": "12 Gy over 2-3 weeks (low-dose) or 30-36 Gy over 6-9 weeks (conventional)", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Limited safety data for concurrent systemic retinoids, HDAC inhibitors, or mogamulizumab", "indication": "Generalized skin involvement (MF/SS)", "key_trial": "", "toxicities": "Skin erythema, desquamation, dryness, edema; eye, hand, foot shielding required; temporary alopecia; nail changes; fatigue"}]}

timeline.title: Curative RT for indolent PCBCL (PCMZL, PCFCL)
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: Conventional fractionation (e.g., 2 Gy per fraction), Fractions: 12 fractions, Schedule: Daily over 2-3 weeks, Indication: Solitary/regional disease

timeline.title: Palliative RT for MF/SS
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: Up to 8 Gy in a single fraction; lower doses per fraction (3-5 Gy) may be preferred, Fractions: 1-4 fractions, Schedule: Over 1-2 weeks, Indication: Individual patches, plaques, or tumors for symptom control

timeline.title: Curative RT for unilesional MF
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: Conventional fractionation, Fractions: 12-15 fractions, Schedule: Over 2-3 weeks, Indication: Rare initial unilesional presentation

timeline.title: RT for PC-ALCL
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: Conventional fractionation, Fractions: Varies, Indication: Solitary or localized lesions

timeline.title: RT for SPTCL
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: Conventional fractionation, Fractions: Varies, Indication: Single lesion or limited disease

timeline.title: Total skin electron beam therapy (TSEBT)
timeline.body: Dose per fraction: 4-6 Gy per week, Fractions: Varies, Schedule: Over 2-9 weeks, Indication: Generalized skin involvement (MF/SS)
